summary: Replayed ADR 0270 from the newest synchronized mainline head by converging the shared Docker publication assurance helper across `docker-runtime-lv3` and `coolify-lv3`, letting the helper recover missing Docker publication chains on Coolify, then using the repo-managed Harbor converge path on the same head to repair the stubborn Harbor publication drift that still surfaced after the shared replay. The final steady state re-verified Harbor public and local ping, Keycloak local and public issuer health, OpenBao local health, Outline local and public health, and Langfuse local health plus sign-in reachability.

verification.observed: `make converge-docker-publication-assurance env=production` succeeded from synchronized commit `6bbe13b66c382d5521cc7b85cc070355558fc326` with final recap `coolify-lv3 : ok=69 changed=5 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=3 rescued=0 ignored=0` and `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=115 changed=3 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=6 rescued=0 ignored=0`.

verification.check: Governed Harbor recovery on the same synchronized head
verification.observed: After the shared replay a focused Harbor diagnostic still showed the stale publication signature (`NetworkSettings.Ports = {}` with no live `8095` listener or `DOCKER` nat rule), so `make converge-harbor env=production` was replayed from the same synchronized tree and completed with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=134 changed=7 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=21 rescued=0 ignored=0` and `nginx-lv3 : ok=39 changed=3 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=11 rescued=0 ignored=0`.

verification.check: Focused regression coverage on the synchronized integrated tree
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest -q tests/test_harbor_runtime_role.py tests/test_keycloak_runtime_role.py tests/test_openbao_compose_env_helper.py tests/test_docker_publication_assurance.py tests/test_docker_publication_assurance_playbook.py tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py tests/test_platform_observation_tool.py tests/test_post_verify_tasks.py tests/test_gotenberg_runtime_role.py tests/test_nextcloud_playbook.py tests/test_nextcloud_runtime_role.py tests/test_nginx_edge_publication_role.py` returned `101 passed in 25.79s` on the final `0.177.94` merged-main tree.

verification.check: Final public and private publication verification after Harbor repair

verification.observed: `LV3_SNAPSHOT_BRANCH=main ./scripts/validate_repo.sh workstream-surfaces agent-standards yaml json data-models ansible-syntax role-argument-specs health-probes generated-docs` completed successfully from the final `0.177.94` merged-main snapshot after refreshing ADR 0270 onto `origin/main` commit `46df65e7f2227ca79a38035d2d24f53b6c02b5f8`.

notes: This receipt is the canonical exact-main proof for ADR 0270 and justifies the platform version bump from the latest realistic pre-replay baseline `0.130.61` to `0.130.62` on repository version `0.177.94`.

The first post-replay probe caught Harbor still exposing configured `HostConfig.PortBindings` without live `NetworkSettings.Ports`; the governed Harbor role's controlled compose reset path resolved that last stubborn publication gap without requiring undocumented manual Docker cleanup.
Keycloak, OpenBao, Outline, and Langfuse all remained healthy through the synchronized replay and the Harbor follow-up recovery.
